content.problemStatement:
Given a singly linked list of integers, reverse the list and return the head of the reversed list. The list is terminated by -1, which is not part of the list.


Input Format

First line contains number of test cases TTT
For each test case:

Single line of space-separated integers ending with -1




Output Format

For each test case, print the reversed list elements separated by spaces, ending with -1.
constraints: -1 appears only as the terminator and never as a node value
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 <= T <= 5), the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing space-separated integers representing the nodes of a singly linked list, terminated by -1. The terminator -1 is not part of the list.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output one line containing the values of the reversed linked list in order, separated by a single space, and terminated by -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The original list 1→2→3→4→5→6 is reversed to 6→5→4→3→2→1.
A single‑node list remains unchanged when reversed.

solution.approach: We read the integers of each test case until the sentinel -1 and build a singly linked list. To reverse the list we use the classic iterative three-pointer technique: maintain prev, curr and next pointers, repeatedly redirect curr->next to prev, and advance the pointers. This runs in linear time and uses only O(1) extra memory. After reversal we traverse the list once more to print the node values followed by the terminator -1. The algorithm is optimal because each node is visited a constant number of times.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of nodes in the list; overall O(total N) for all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the linked-list nodes (input storage). The reversal itself uses O(1) auxiliary space.

code.Python3:
import sys

def reverse(head):
    prev=None
    curr=head
    while curr:
        nxt=curr[1]
        curr[1]=prev
        prev=curr
        curr=nxt
    return prev

def build(vals):
    head=tail=None
    for v in vals:
        node=[v,None]
        if head is None:
            head=tail=node
        else:
            tail[1]=node
            tail=node
    return head

def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        vals=[]
        for token in it:
            num=int(token)
            if num==-1:
                break
            vals.append(num)
        head=build(vals)
        head=reverse(head)
        parts=[]
        cur=head
        while cur:
            parts.append(str(cur[0]))
            cur=cur[1]
        parts.append('-1')
        out.append(' '.join(parts))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
